name: Tom Brown's School Days
author: Hughes, Thomas
body:
Yeast; a Problem. 12mo. $1.75.

          Hereward, the Last of the English. 12mo. $1.75

          The Water Babies. A Fairy Tale for a Land Baby.
          With Illustrations. 12mo. $1.75.

          The Heroes; Or, Greek Fairy Tales for my Children.
          With Illustrations. 12mo. $1.75.

          Glaucus; Or, the Wonders of the Shore. With
          Colored Illustrations. 12mo. $1.75.

          Madam How and Lady Why; Or, First Lessons in Earth
          Lore for Children. With Illustrations. 12mo.
          $1.75.

          Town Geology, 12mo. $1.75.




CHARLOTTE M. YONGE'S NOVELS AND TALES.

          New uniform Illustrated edition. 12mo. Cloth, gilt.
          Per volume, $1.75.


          The Heir of Redclyffe.
               Heartsease.
                   The Daisy Chain.

          The Trial. More Links of the Daisy Chain.
              Hopes and Fears.
                   Dynevor Terrace.
                       The Young Stepmother.

          The Dove in the Eagle's Nest.
              The Caged Lion.
                   The Chaplet of Pearls.

          The Pillars of the House. Two volumes
               My Young Alcides.
                   The Three Brides.
                       Magnum Bonum.

          The Danvers Papers and Lady Hester.
               Womankind.

***MACMILLAN & CO'S complete classified catalogue sent free by mail to
any address on receipt of six cents.

          MACMILLAN & CO.
             22 Bond Street, New York.



Transcriber's Notes:

In the list of illustrations, it is noted that "CHAIRING TOM IN THE
QUADRANGLE" is on page 366. In fact this illustration was used as the
frontispiece in the original text. This has been retained and in the
HTML version, clicking on the number will take the reader to the
frontispiece.

Obvious punctuation errors have been corrected.

Page 66, "t e" changed to "the" (in the dark for)

Page 94, "talkiug" changed to "talking" (rest talking in whispers)

Page 260, " a nd" changed to "head; and" (head; and to convert)

Page 266, "misletoe" changed to "mistletoe" (lump of mistletoe)

Page 365, "indifferent" changed to "in different" (so had other boys in
different)
